skill.name: wiki-changelog
skill.description: Analyzes git commit history and generates structured changelogs categorized by change type. Use when the user asks about recent changes, wants a changelog, or needs to understand what changed in the codebase.
skill.summary: Generates structured changelogs from git history by analyzing commits, grouping by time period, and categorizing by change type.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 4 high-risk patterns related to 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3, 22, 27, and 32. This is a FALSE POSITIVE - the skill contains no cryptographic code. The analyzer misidentified common words in the markdown documentation. The skill is a simple changelog generator that reads git log data and produces structured output. No network calls, no external commands, no file writes, no environment access.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (FALSE POSITIVE)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' but this skill contains no cryptographic code. Manual review confirms this is a false positive - the file contains only markdown documentation describing git changelog generation procedures. No encryption, hashing, or cryptographic functions present.

content.value_statement: Manually compiling changelogs from git history is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automatically analyzes commit history and generates structured, categorized changelogs organized by change type and time period.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes git commit history to extract all commits with dates, authors, and messages
Groups commits by time period: recent commits (last 7 days) and older commits
Categorizes commits by type: Features, Fixes, Refactoring, Docs, Config, Dependencies, Breaking Changes
Generates concise, user-facing descriptions using project terminology
Highlights breaking changes prominently with migration notes
limitations: Only works with git repositories - requires access to git history
Cannot determine change impact without context from commit messages
Depends on well-written commit messages for accurate categorization
Does not generate release artifacts or publish changelogs automatically

use_cases.title: Generate Release Notes
use_cases.description: Quickly produce release notes by analyzing recent commits and categorizing all changes since the last release.

use_cases.title: Track Project Activity
use_cases.description: Understand what has changed in a project over time by viewing a structured summary of recent development activity.

use_cases.title: Onboard Team Members
use_cases.description: Help new team members understand recent changes and the current state of the project through organized changelogs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Changelog Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the wiki-changelog skill to generate a changelog for this repository. What changes have been made recently?
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to know recent changes

prompt_templates.title: Release Notes Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a changelog for version X.X.X release. Include all features, bug fixes, and any breaking changes.

prompt_templates.title: Weekly Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the git history and provide a weekly summary of all changes, grouped by category like features, fixes, and documentation updates.

prompt_templates.title: Breaking Changes Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a changelog that highlights all breaking changes and includes migration notes for upgrading.

output_examples.input: Generate a changelog for the past week
output_examples.output:
## Changelog - Last 7 Days

### Features 🆕
- Add user authentication flow (2024-01-15)
- Implement password reset functionality (2024-01-14)

### Fixes 🐛
- Resolve login redirect issue on mobile (2024-01-13)
- Fix timezone handling in date picker (2024-01-12)

### Documentation 📝
- Update API documentation with new endpoints (2024-01-11)

### Dependencies 📦
- Upgrade React to v18.2.0 (2024-01-10)

output_examples.input: What changed in the last month? Include breaking changes.
output_examples.output:
## Changelog - Last 30 Days

### Breaking Changes ⚠️
- **Migration Required**: Rename `User.getProfile()` to `User.profile()` - update all references
- **Migration Required**: API v2 endpoints now require authentication header

### Features 🆕
- Multiple new features listed...

### Fixes 🐛
- Multiple bug fixes listed...

best_practices: Ensure commit messages follow a consistent convention (Conventional Commits recommended) for accurate categorization
Review generated changelogs before publishing to ensure terminology matches project standards
Use the skill regularly (weekly or before each release) to maintain up-to-date changelogs
anti_patterns: Do not rely solely on automated categorization - verify that commits are grouped correctly
Avoid using vague commit messages like 'fix', 'update', 'changes' which reduce changelog usefulness
Do not skip reviewing breaking changes - always verify migration notes are accurate

faq.question: Does this skill write files to my repository?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reads git history and generates changelog output. It does not modify any files.

faq.question: How does the skill categorize commits?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes commit messages and classifies them based on keywords: Features (feat, add), Fixes (fix, bug), Refactoring (refactor), Docs (docs), Config (config), Dependencies (deps), and Breaking (break).

faq.question: Can I customize the categories?
faq.answer: Currently the categories are predefined. You can modify the skill code to add custom categories if needed.

faq.question: What if my commit messages are not descriptive?
faq.answer: The quality of the changelog depends on commit message quality. Encourage team members to write descriptive commit messages for better results.

faq.question: Does this work with git submodules?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes the main repository history. Submodule changes would need to be tracked separately.

faq.question: Can I generate changelogs for specific time periods?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill groups commits into daily (last 7 days) and weekly (older) periods by default. You can request specific date ranges in your prompt.
